name: Culebra Castle Trail
name_original: Castillo de Alange – circular con vistas al pantano
description: Hike the moderate 3.3-mile Castillo de Alange circular trail for panoramic reservoir views and a glimpse into history at the castle ruins.
region_intro: Hiking around Alange offers diverse terrain characterized by the expansive Embalse de Alange reservoir and surrounding hills. The region features limestone soil, diverse arboreal species, and areas like Sierra Peñas Blancas and Valle de la Jabata. Prominent landmarks include the Cerro del Castillo de la Culebra, home to the historic Alange Castle, providing elevated views. This landscape supports varied flora and fauna, making it suitable for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Alange The most popular hiking route is Culebra Castle Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Alange Reservoir and leads to the historic castle ruins. Another top favourite among local hikers is Views of the Alange Reservoir – Alange Castle loop from Alange, an easy 2.0 miles (3.2 km) path. This trail provides scenic views of the reservoir and the Alange Castle. Local hikers also love the Viewpoint of Alange Reservoir loop from La Zarza, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through the hills surrounding the reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Alange is defined by reservoir views, historic castle hills, and diverse natural areas like the Valle de la Jabata. The network offers options for easy strolls to more challenging routes. The routes in Alange are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Alange's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Alange?
A: Alange offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore the Embalse de Alange reservoir, surrounding hills, and historical sites.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Alange?
A: Yes, Alange has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3958831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir – Alange Castle loop from Alange&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.0-mile (3.2 km) path offering scenic views of the reservoir and the castle without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Alange?
A: Many of the hiking routes in Alange are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991590294/culebra-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culebra Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.3-mile (5.3 km) circular route that provides stunning reservoir views and leads to the historic castle ruins. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Alange Reservoir loop from La Zarza&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.6-mile (5.8 km) loop.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Alange?
A: Hiking in Alange offers a rich natural experience centered around the Embalse de Alange reservoir. You'll encounter diverse landscapes including limestone hills, the Sierra Peñas Blancas, and the Valle de la Jabata, which features ancient Moorish orchards. The area is home to various arboreal species like rockrose, wild olive, and kermes oak, and supports a wide array of birds such as the Spanish Imperial Eagle and Griffon Vulture.

Q: Are there historical landmarks to explore on the trails?
A: Absolutely. A prominent historical landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt;, an Arab fortress perched on the Cerro del Castillo de la Culebra. Many trails lead to its ruins, offering panoramic views. Alange is also renowned for its splendid Roman Thermal Baths, a UNESCO World Heritage site, which can be explored after your hike.

Q: Is Alange a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Alange offers several routes suitable for families. The easy trails, particularly those bordering the reservoir or with minimal elevation, are great for all ages. The natural beauty and historical sites like the castle can make for an engaging outing for children.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alange?
A: The best time for hiking in Alange is typically during spring, autumn, and winter. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making for more comfortable conditions to explore the trails and enjoy the natural beauty without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there options for public transport or parking near the trailheads?
A: While specific public transport options directly to all trailheads might be limited, Alange town itself is accessible. Parking is generally available in and around the town, especially near popular starting points for routes leading to the castle or reservoir. It's advisable to check local information for the most convenient parking spots for your chosen route.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alange?
A: The hiking trails in Alange are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning views of the reservoir, the historical significance of the castle, and the diverse natural landscapes found along the routes.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes in the area?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Alange does offer longer and more demanding routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir – Alange Castle loop from La Zarza&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.3 km (13.9 miles) trail with significant elevation gain, providing a full day's adventure through the region's hills.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other activities in Alange?
A: Absolutely. The Embalse de Alange reservoir is a hub for water sports like canoeing, windsurfing, and sailing. After a hike, you can also visit the historic Roman Thermal Baths for relaxation or explore the cultural heritage of Alange town.

Q: Are there specific routes that offer great views of the Alange Reservoir?
A: Many routes in Alange are designed to showcase the stunning Embalse de Alange. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991590294/culebra-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culebra Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3958831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir – Alange Castle loop from Alange&lt;/a&gt; both offer excellent panoramic views of the reservoir, often from elevated positions near the castle.
tips: At the top of the Culebra hill is the Alange castle, a strategic fortress from the 9th century and the site of battles for Arabs and Christians.

The Alange Reservoir is an impressive engineering work located in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, near the town of Alange and about 20 km from Mérida.
The concrete dam was inaugurated in 1992 and is located on the Matachel River, close to its mouth in the Guadiana River.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular trail usually takes around 1 hour and 37 minutes to complete. However, this can vary depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the views and exploring the castle ruins.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail to Castillo de Alange?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated on the rocky Cerro de la Culebra, featuring steep and abrupt quartzite slopes. You'll encounter some steep ascents, particularly if you take the route via Puerta del Sol, which may have exposed sections. While generally accessible, some parts require sure-footedness. You can choose between paths that are more exposed to the sun or those offering shade.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main highlights are the historic Alange Castle, offering a glimpse into Roman, Moorish, and medieval history, and the stunning Views of the Alange Reservoir. From the castle summit, you'll enjoy panoramic 360-degree vistas of the reservoir, the surrounding landscape, Alange village, and even distant castles like Hornachos and Montemolín.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Alange trail suitable for beginners or families with small children?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, some sections of the trail involve uphill climbs and require sure-footedness. It's generally suitable for most fitness levels, but it's not recommended for individuals with knee, hip, or general agility problems. For very small children, especially on the south-facing slope, accessibility can be a concern.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The route is best enjoyed outside of the hot summer months. Spring and autumn offer more pleasant temperatures for hiking and exploring the exposed sections of the trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the village of Alange, which serves as the base for accessing the trail. From there, signs or local guidance will direct you to the start of the circular route up to the castle.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this natural area are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the steep sections and potential wildlife. Be mindful of the rocky terrain and sun exposure, particularly during warmer periods.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or visit the castle ruins. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this circular route overlap with other local paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the "Callejón de los Frailes" and the "Camino de la Culebra" during your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is part of the Natura 2000 Network, recognized for its environmental and ornithological value. It's a good spot for birdwatching, with species like the Golden Eagle, Griffon Vulture, and Bonelli's Eagle potentially visible. Keep an eye out for other local fauna as well.

faqs.question: Are there any specific strategies or tips for hiking this route?
faqs.answer: Consider starting your hike earlier in the day, especially outside of peak summer, to avoid the midday sun on exposed sections. While the trail is circular, some hikers find certain ascents more challenging; be prepared for steep climbs. Don't forget to bring plenty of water, as there are no facilities on the trail itself.
